description: The crate-level feature table (lib.rs lines 12-17) lists the rsa-pss feature with signature type rsa::pss::Signature, with no mention of a key-size restriction. A caller enabling the rsa-pss feature and passing a RsaPss4096Sha256 backend (a plausible future algorithm — SigningAlgorithm is #[non_exhaustive]) will get SecretError::AlgorithmMismatch at RsaPssSigner::new() with no indication from the feature name or the table that 2048-bit is a hard constraint. The struct doc (line 222) does state '2048-bit RSA keys producing 256-byte signatures' but the feature table is the first thing callers read. Fix: add a key-size note to the feature table row for rsa-pss, e.g. change 'rsa::pss::Signature' to 'rsa::pss::Signature (2048-bit only)'. Also consider naming the type RsaPss2048Signer to make the constraint self-documenting in the type name.
title: rsa-pss feature name implies any RSA-PSS key; actually locked to 2048-bit
